title: Ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel takes effect
subhead: A 10-day ceasefire agreement between the two countries took effect on Thursday, as President Trump said he was trying to set up the first-ever meeting between the leaders of Lebanon and Israel.

Pope Leo XIV denounces 'a handful of tyrants' destroying the world during visit to Cameroon
subhead: The pope continued his tour of the Central African country with a visit to Bamenda, the capital of the conflict-ridden Northwest Region of Cameroon. He delivered a message of peace and condemned acts of war across the world.

title: 'Cesar Chavez was no friend to the undocumented whom ICE now persecutes'
subhead: After recent revelations by 'The New York Times' of rape and sexual abuse cast a shadow over the legacy of the renowned American labor leader, American academic Sarah Quesada questions the glorifying of Chavez.

title: Rudolf Nureyev's ballet 'Roméo et Juliette' returns to the Opéra national de Paris
subhead: The spectacular production gives the new generation of dancers a chance to showcase their talents.

title: 'After Greenland and Iran, the Hungarian election marks a new step in the transatlantic rift'
subhead: The crushing electoral defeat of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, despite massive support from Washington, underscores the failure of Donald Trump's ideological campaign in Europe. The gap is widening inexorably, writes Le Monde columnist Sylvie Kauffmann.

subhead: The reactionary American blogger close to the Trumpian figures was invited to a symposium in Germany with prominent scholars. Some critics say the invitation signals that red lines have vanished from intellectual debate.

title: Firing of respected French publisher demonstrates billionaire Bolloré's tightening grip
subhead: Olivier Nora, the long-time CEO of the Grasset publishing house, was fired on Tuesday. He was replaced with a close associate of Vincent Bolloré, who took control of France's leading publishing group Hachette Livre in 2023.

title: 'At that price, how can we accept?' Trump Organization's Vietnam golf project sparks protests and corruption allegations
subhead: Signed just before the 2024 US presidential election, the project has caused divisions within the government and sparked anger among local residents, who have been offered extremely low compensation for the seizure of their land.
